python3 -c "
import numpy as np
base = '/home/mrsar/protein-ag/past-published-data/disease-relevant non-inhibited/'
# Look at a few representative datasets
paths = [
    'Abeta/Meisl2014/Ab40_sec_IN_HOURS/fit.tsv',
    'Alphasyn_Gaspar2017/03uM_seed all/fit.tsv',
    'htt/Kar2011/Fits/Kar2011_Q37_sec/fit.tsv',
]
for p in paths:
    data = np.loadtxt(base+p, delimiter='\t', skiprows=1)
    with open(base+p) as f:
        header = f.readline().strip().split('\t')
    print(f'{p}')
    print(f'  header: {header}')
    print(f'  shape: {data.shape}')
    print(f'  time range: {data[:,0].min():.3f} - {data[:,0].max():.3f}')
    print(f'  y range per col: {[(round(data[:,i].min(),3), round(data[:,i].max(),3)) for i in range(1, min(4, data.shape[1]))]}')
    print()
"

import numpy as np
base = '/home/mrsar/protein-ag/past-published-data/disease-relevant non-inhibited/'
# Check datasets with only 1-2 curves (no validation contribution)
# vs datasets with 3+ curves (validation matters)
import os
for root, dirs, files in os.walk(base):
    for f in files:
        if f == 'fit.tsv':
            path = os.path.join(root, f)
            with open(path) as fh:
                header = fh.readline().strip().split('\t')
            ncols = len(header) - 1  # subtract time column
            rel = path.replace(base, '')
            print(f'{ncols} curves: {rel}')
"
